config_files.notes: Main user settings. Overwritten by the in-game Graphics menu on every exit. Do not rely on manual edits surviving a subsequent in-game settings change.

config_files.path_note: ReFrame resolves %STEAM_LIBRARY% from Steam's libraryfolders.vdf. Typical default path: C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Cyberpunk 2077\engine\config\platform\pc\. Create this file if it does not exist — do not edit existing engine INIs in this directory.
Default GOG install path. Create this file if absent.

keys.effect: Multiplier for texture and geometry streaming distance. Increasing this reduces asset pop-in in Night City at the cost of VRAM and CPU streaming time.
keys.notes: Keep quality-mode value at 1.3 rather than 1.5 for Cyberpunk specifically — the large open world means higher values cause measurable CPU stutter on mid-range hardware even at low GPU load.

keys.effect: Distance at which NPCs receive full physics rig simulation for cloth, hair, and accessories.
keys.notes: Default is 3. Most impactful in crowd-heavy areas such as Market Street and Kabuki. Values above 6 provide negligible visual benefit.

keys.effect: Screen-space reflection quality for wet surfaces, puddles, and polished floors: 0=off, 1=low, 2=medium, 3=high.
keys.notes: Has no effect when ray tracing reflections or path tracing is enabled in the in-game Graphics menu — those renderers fully replace SSR.
Has no effect when ray tracing ambient occlusion or path tracing is enabled in-game. Disabling (0) is a safe performance win on low/mid hardware without ray tracing.

keys.effect: Volumetric fog render resolution: 0=low, 1=medium, 2=high. Affects the quality of fog, atmospheric haze, and god rays throughout Night City.
keys.notes: Fog is visually prominent in many Night City districts. Reducing to 0 is one of the highest per-FPS-gain tweaks on mid/low hardware with minimal visual impact from normal play distances.

engine_overrides.behaviour: The entire UserSettings.json file is rewritten by the game whenever the player closes the in-game Graphics or Gameplay settings menu. Manual edits to this file will be lost if the user subsequently opens and saves any in-game setting.
engine_overrides.action: Warn the user before editing UserSettings.json. Recommend engine override INI keys for persistent tweaks. For settings only exposed in UserSettings.json (resolution, upscaler mode), instruct the user to make changes in-game rather than via file edit.

engine_overrides.behaviour: Patch 2.0 (September 2023) added an in-game Crowd Density slider under Settings > Gameplay. This slider controls crowd-related SpawnSystem values and overwrites any manual SpawnSystem INI entries on load.
engine_overrides.action: Do not recommend SpawnSystem INI keys. Direct users to the in-game Crowd Density slider instead.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: DLSS Quality on RTX cards; FSR Quality on AMD/Intel. Use DLSS Performance at 1440p+ on mid-range RTX hardware. Frame Generation (DLSS 3) requires RTX 40 series; Multi Frame Generation (DLSS 4) requires RTX 50 series.
manual_only_settings.reason: Upscaler selection and mode are only configurable through the in-game Graphics menu and stored in UserSettings.json.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Disable all RT on Low/Mid hardware. Enable RT Local Shadows + AO on mid-high (RTX 3070+). Full Path Tracing (Overdrive) only on RTX 4070 Ti Super+ with DLSS Quality enabled.
manual_only_settings.reason: RT features are toggled via the in-game Graphics menu. At 4K native resolution, even RTX 4090 cannot maintain 60 FPS with Path Tracing — DLSS Quality is required.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Low on performance hardware; Medium on balanced; High on quality. Has meaningful CPU frametime impact in busy districts.
manual_only_settings.reason: Added in patch 2.0. Directly controls NPC population density and overrides legacy SpawnSystem INI keys.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Default 70°. Increase to 80–90° if prone to motion discomfort in first-person view.
manual_only_settings.reason: The game is primarily first-person; narrow FOV is a common trigger for motion sickness.

notes: Cyberpunk 2077 ships with an older DLSS DLL. Users with RTX cards can replace nvngx_dlss.dll in the game install root with a newer version for improved upscaling quality and access to DLSS 3 Ray Reconstruction (2.0+) or DLSS 4 Multi Frame Generation (RTX 50 series). DLSS Swapper automates this.
Engine override INI files created in engine\config\platform\pc\ survive patches and do not need to be recreated after updates.
Pre-patch 2.0 optimisation guides (before September 2023) often reference SpawnSystem INI keys for crowd density. These are now overridden by the in-game Crowd Density slider and should be ignored.

The game received major performance optimisations in patches 1.5 (February 2022), 1.6 (September 2022), and 2.0 (September 2023). Benchmark data from before patch 1.5 is not representative of current performance.
